steps.title: Find the smallest maximum depth
steps.content: Scan the "Maximum depth" column for all four species. The values are 115, 5, 30, and 25 meters. The smallest is 5 meters, which belongs to Acropora bushyensis, found in Indo-Pacific waters.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A maximum depth of 5 meters is less than 30 meters, so it falls in the shallow zone, not the mesophotic zone (30–150 m). Check each option:
  • Choice A — 'Indo-Pacific waters in the shallow zone': Acropora bushyensis has the smallest maximum depth (5 m) and lives in Indo-Pacific waters; 5 m is in the shallow zone. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Caribbean waters in the shallow zone': The species with the smallest maximum depth is in Indo-Pacific waters, not Caribbean waters. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Indo-Pacific waters in the mesophotic zone': 5 meters is well above 30 m, so it falls in the shallow zone, not the mesophotic zone. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Caribbean waters in the mesophotic zone': Wrong region and wrong zone. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
